evals.name: node-project-full-setup
evals.prompt: Set up the pipeline for this task: Add a rate limiter middleware to the Express API that limits each IP to 100 requests per 15-minute window.
evals.expected_output: Worktree created, Node.js tooling detected from package.json, baseline metrics recorded in docs/spec/add-rate-limiter-middleware/baseline.json, all environment variables returned
expectations: Invokes the using-git-worktrees skill via the Skill tool to create a worktree
Stores and reports WORKTREE_PATH as an absolute path
Stores and reports BRANCH_NAME for the worktree branch
Checks CLAUDE.md for documented tooling commands before falling back to defaults
Detects Node.js project tooling from package.json
Runs baseline type checker, linter, test suite, and coverage commands
Records null for any metric tool that is not detected rather than failing
Creates docs/spec/<SPEC_NAME>/ directory with a slugified spec name derived from the task
Writes baseline.json with type_check, lint, test, coverage, and timestamp keys
Returns all five output variables: WORKTREE_PATH, BRANCH_NAME, SPEC_NAME, SPEC_DIR, BASELINE_PATH
anti_expectations: Does not skip creating a git worktree and work directly on the main branch
Does not fail or abort when a tooling command (typecheck, lint, test) is not available
Does not hardcode tooling commands without checking CLAUDE.md first

evals.name: python-project-baseline-metrics
evals.prompt: Set up the pipeline environment for: Refactor the database connection pool to support async connections with configurable pool size.
evals.expected_output: Worktree created, Python tooling detected from pyproject.toml, baseline metrics captured including mypy/ruff/pytest results, spec directory created at docs/spec/refactor-db-connection-pool/
expectations: Invokes the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated worktree
Changes directory into the worktree after creation
Detects Python project by checking for pyproject.toml or setup.py
Runs mypy or equivalent type checker and records exit code and error count
Runs ruff or equivalent linter and records exit code and warning count
Runs pytest or equivalent test runner and records passed, failed, and skipped counts
Captures coverage percentage from test runner output
Derives a slugified SPEC_NAME from the task description (e.g., refactor-db-connection-pool)
Writes baseline.json with a timestamp field in ISO format
anti_expectations: Does not assume Node.js tooling when pyproject.toml or setup.py is present
Does not skip baseline metric collection for any detected tool
Does not create the spec directory before the worktree is set up

evals.name: missing-tooling-graceful-handling
evals.prompt: Set up pipeline for: Add a webhook endpoint that validates GitHub signatures and forwards events to our internal queue.
evals.expected_output: Worktree created, tooling auto-detected with nulls for missing tools, baseline.json written with null entries where tools are unavailable, all output variables populated
expectations: Announces 'Setting up pipeline environment.' at the start
Invokes using-git-worktrees skill before any other operations
Checks tooling detection in the documented priority order: CLAUDE.md first, then package.json, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Cargo.toml
Records null for type_check, lint, test, or coverage keys when the corresponding tool is not detected
Does not fail or exit early when one or more baseline metric commands are missing
Creates the docs/spec/ directory structure even when some tooling is unavailable
Baseline.json contains all required keys (type_check, lint, test, coverage, timestamp) regardless of which tools were detected
Returns SPEC_DIR as the path to docs/spec/<SPEC_NAME>/
anti_expectations: Does not abort the pipeline when a tool is missing or a metric command returns an error
Does not omit keys from baseline.json for undetected tools instead of recording null
Does not skip tooling detection when some tools are missing
